genre_refs: Theodore Roosevelt equestrian portrait (Tade Styka 'Colonel Roosevelt' painting)
description: A propaganda/slogan poster built on a classic oil painting of Theodore Roosevelt on horseback (the well-known equestrian portrait of Roosevelt as a Rough Rider, rendered in painterly brushwork). The image shows a mustachioed Roosevelt in a tan/khaki riding uniform and broad-brimmed campaign hat, mounted on a dark rearing/galloping horse, set against a stormy grey-blue sky and dusty ground. Across the bottom, a translucent dark band carries large white serif capital text: 'PATRIOTISM, NOT GLOBALISM'. A small circular gold seal (appears to be a U.S. government / Department of Labor style emblem) is centered just above the slogan. The aesthetic fuses heroic Americana fine-art imagery with a punchy modern political slogan. Messaging technique: invokes an iconic muscular-Americana president to brand the administration's economic nationalism / anti-globalist 'America First' stance; the rugged horseback imagery signals strength, tradition, and rugged independence. Posted by the Department of Labor account paired with text about never letting 'the globalists' control the government. Target audience: nationalist/America First supporters, general public.
